classes.name: Aphid
classes.description: Small sap-sucking insects that cluster on leaves and stems, causing yellowing, curling, and reduced growth. Severe infestations may reduce grain yield. Use neem oil, insecticidal soap, or aphid-specific insecticides.

classes.name: Black Rust / Stem Rust
classes.description: A fungal disease causing reddish-brown to black elongated pustules on stems and leaves. Severe infections weaken plants and reduce yield. Use resistant varieties and fungicides when necessary.

classes.name: Brown Rust / Leaf Rust
classes.description: Fungal disease producing orange-brown pustules mainly on leaf surfaces. Reduces photosynthesis and grain production. Apply fungicides and grow resistant wheat varieties.

classes.name: Common Root Rot
classes.description: A soil-borne fungal disease causing dark lesions on roots and stem bases, leading to weak growth and poor nutrient uptake. Improve drainage and rotate crops.

classes.name: Fusarium Head Blight / Scab
classes.description: A fungal infection affecting wheat heads, causing bleaching and shriveled grains. Can produce harmful mycotoxins. Use resistant varieties and apply fungicides during flowering.

classes.name: Helminthosporium Leaf Blight / Leaf Blight
classes.description: Causes elongated brown lesions on leaves, reducing photosynthesis and plant vigor. More common in warm and humid conditions. Apply fungicides and maintain field sanitation.

classes.name: Mite
classes.description: Microscopic pests feeding on plant sap, causing yellowing, curling, and stunted growth. Heavy infestations may significantly reduce crop health. Use miticides or biological predators.

classes.name: Powdery Mildew
classes.description: Fungal disease appearing as white powdery patches on leaves and stems. Can reduce photosynthesis and yield. Improve airflow and apply fungicides if needed.

classes.name: Septoria Leaf Blotch
classes.description: Causes irregular brown lesions with black specks on leaves, reducing green leaf area and grain filling. Crop rotation and fungicides help control spread.

classes.name: Smut (Loose, Flag)
classes.description: Fungal disease where grain heads are replaced by black powdery spores. Often seed-borne. Use certified treated seeds and resistant cultivars.

classes.name: Spot Blotch
classes.description: Fungal disease producing oval dark brown spots on leaves, especially under warm humid conditions. Can significantly reduce yield. Apply fungicides and maintain healthy crop nutrition.

classes.name: Stem Fly
classes.description: Larvae bore into wheat stems causing dead hearts, stunted growth, and poor tillering. Use resistant varieties and insecticides where infestation is severe.

classes.name: Tan Spot
classes.description: Fungal disease causing tan-colored lesions with yellow halos on leaves. Reduces photosynthesis and crop yield. Crop rotation and fungicide sprays are effective management strategies.

classes.name: Wheat Blast
classes.description: A highly destructive fungal disease causing bleaching of spikes and shriveled grains. Spreads rapidly under humid conditions. Early fungicide application and resistant cultivars are essential.

classes.name: Yellow Rust / Stripe Rust
classes.description: Fungal disease producing yellow-orange stripe-like pustules on leaves. Thrives in cool, moist weather and reduces yield significantly. Use resistant varieties and fungicides.
